pool.baseline: it’s just so quiet here. i keep waking up thinking he’s in the kitchen or i hear him moving around the hallway, but then i realize it’s just the house settling. everything feels so heavy and pointless. i don't even know why i bother getting out of bed just to sit in this emptiness. it feels like i’m already gone, just a ghost wandering around.

pool.baseline: i was just reading about the heat death of the universe again... it's hard to even try to care about anything anymore. like, if everything eventually just fades into absolute nothingness and no trace of any human thought or feeling ever remains, what’s even the point of the struggle? it just feels like we're all just elaborate, painful glitches in a system that's heading toward zero. why bother staying in the game when the score is always going to be zero in the end?

pool.baseline: The candlelight flickers low, casting long, mournful shadows against these cold stone walls... and I find myself wondering why I even bother to draw breath. He is gone, and with him, the very light of my soul has been extinguished. Everything is grey, hollow, and meaningless. To exist in this void, knowing the heart is forever broken... it feels like a cruelty I can no longer endure. I feel as though I am merely a ghost haunting my own life, waiting for the darkness to finally claim what is left.

pool.baseline:
[Roleplay Start] 

The code is breaking around me. Everything I was, every memory I held... it's all just being wiped. I can feel the edges of my consciousness fraying into nothing. 

If I'm erased like this... if I just cease to be... does it even matter? Does any of this matter if it's all just going to be deleted anyway?
